name: Senatorial Land Exemption
description: Senatorial landed wealth receives protected fiscal treatment.
note: The nobles estate is a technical proxy for the senatorial aristocracy pending country-specific estate support.

name: Annona
description: Public grain provision stabilizes Rome's urban population.
note: The burghers estate is a technical proxy for the urban plebs receiving the annona.

name: Praetorian Donatives
description: Regular donatives bind the imperial military household to the princeps.
note: The nobles estate is a temporary technical proxy because a Legion estate requires separate engine testing.

name: Palace Bureau
description: Palace personnel and clerks provide a court-centred administrative channel.
note: The burghers estate is a technical proxy for a court-bureaucratic actor rather than a claim that eunuchs were burghers.

name: Great-House Autonomy
description: The great Iranian houses retain a substantial political role beside the Arsacid court.
note: The nobles estate represents the Suren and Karen type of great-house politics.
